name: Antebellum Rice Bread
recipeInstructions:
This recipe was given to me by an old friend in Savannah and is a favorite breakfast bread from the plantations.
Salt may be omitted if desired.
1. Place melted fats and cooked rice in a heavy saucepan covered with low heat.
2. In a separate bowl, mix cornmeal with salt and cayenne pepper (if using).
3. Whisk together milk and beaten eggs; gradually add to cornmeal mixture.
4. Blend the egg mixture into the rice and fat mixture.
5. Pour batter into a greased 8- or 9-inch square pan.
6. Bake at 450°F until firm when tapped with a finger and golden brown on top, about 25 minutes.
7. Cut into squares to serve.
Makes 1 to 1 1/2 dozen squares.
